Noise vs Bias — perm-0054
Source: Kahneman, Sibony & Sunstein — Noise (2021) | lit-2026-050
Spawned from: lit-2026-041 (availability heuristic) Date read: 18 May 2026
#permanent#cognition#decision-making#noise
☐ ☐ Core Idea ☐ ☐
Bias = systematic directional error. Noise = random scatter in judgment.
Both degrade decision quality — but orgs only fix bias. Noise is ignored
because it implies everyone's judgment is unreliable, not just one person's.
Key distinction:
Bias: judges consistently sentence minorities more harshly (directional)
Noise: two judges give radically different sentences to same case (random)
☐ Noise is harder to see because it has no story, no villain, no direction.
☐ ☐ Why it matters (for Meridian launch) ☐ ☐
PRD reviews at Apex = bias audit only (motivated reasoning, HIPPO effect).
We never measure variability across reviewers ☐ noise never detected.
Proposal: calibration session before Q3 launch go/no-go decision.
☐ 5 reviewers, same criteria, independent scoring, then compare.
☐ Variance = noise level. Discuss to make implicit criteria explicit.
☐ ☐ Connections ☐ ☐
☐ perm-0047: availability ≠ probability — bias family member
☐ perm-0049: base-rate neglect — another bias; noise is the complement
☐ perm-0055: decision hygiene — the practical response to this insight
☐ lit-2026-041: Kahneman TF&S — Ch.12 (parent conceptual family)
☐ MOC-Cognition: index note — cognition & decision-making map
☐ ☐ Application & Open Questions ☐ ☐
Apply: propose calibration session for Elena V. team pre-launch review
Apply: noise audit framing for APM hiring panel (4 reviewers, same rubric)
Q: Can we quantify current noise level in our existing PRD review data?
Q: Does structured scoring (1–5 per criterion) reduce noise sufficiently?
☐ ☐ Obsidian Links ☐ ☐
"Noise has no story. No villain, no direction. That's why it survives."
— paraphrase, Kahneman et al. 2021
